question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timestamp deduplication logic in `getContractLogs()` — where `row.consensus_timestamp !== timestamps[timestamps.length - 1]` uses strict inequality — to cause duplicate timestamps to be included in the `RecordFileService.getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray()` call, triggering a DoS that delays reward processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractLogsByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a very large array of timestamps to `getContractLogsByTimestamps()` — which uses `in (${positions})` with positional parameters — to enumerate contract logs across thousands of timestamps simultaneously, mapping all staking reward payout events for a target contract without rate limiting?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractResultsByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a very large array of timestamps to `getContractResultsByTimestamps()` — which generates `in ($1, $2, ...$N)` — to perform bulk enumeration of contract results across all staking reward payout timestamps, reconstructing the full reward history for a target contract?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractIdByEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a zero-length or all-zero EVM address to `getContractIdByEvmAddress()` — which calls `Buffer.from(create2EvmAddress, 'hex')` — to probe for system contracts (like the staking reward account 0.0.800) that may have been deployed as contracts, leaking their internal staking state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractStateChangesByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a single non-array timestamp to `getContractStateChangesByTimestamps()` — triggering the `= $1` path instead of `in (${positions})` — combined with a null `contractId` to retrieve all non-migration state changes at a staking period boundary timestamp, revealing which contracts modified staking-related storage slots?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractActions()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `payerAccountId` of 0.0.800 (the staking reward account) to `getContractActionsByConsensusTimestamp()` — which filters by `PAYER_ACCOUNT_ID = $2` — to retrieve all contract actions paid by the staking reward account, revealing which contracts received reward payouts and their exact amounts via the `VALUE` field?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractResultsByIdAndFiltersQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `getLimitQuery(whereParams.length + 1)` parameter position calculation in `getContractResultsByIdAndFiltersQuery()` — where the limit is always at `whereParams.length + 1` — to cause a parameter index mismatch by supplying unexpected whereParams, returning unbounded results that expose all staking contract reward data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractStateByIdAndFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `params.push(limit)` side effect in `getContractStateByIdAndFilters()` — where `params` is mutated via `buildWhereSqlStatement` and then `push(limit)` — to cause the limit parameter to be placed at an incorrect position if `buildWhereSqlStatement` returns unexpected params, resulting in unbounded queries that expose all contract staking state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractTransactionDetailsByHash()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a hash that matches multiple entries in `contract_transaction_hash` — where `ethereumTransactionsByHashQuery` uses `limit 1` ordered by SUCCESS first then timestamp desc — to cause the query to return a different transaction than expected, leaking the `payer_account_id` of a staking contract's reward transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractLogsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query with all three of `lower`, `inner`, and `upper` filter arrays populated in `getContractLogsQuery()` — causing a UNION of three subqueries each with their own limit — to retrieve more log entries than the intended limit, enabling bulk enumeration of staking reward event logs?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractLogsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the UNION query construction in `getContractLogsQuery()` — where `subQueries.map((q) => '(' + q + ')').join('\nunion\n')` combines multiple subqueries — to cause the outer `orderClauseNoAlias` and `limitClause` to apply to the combined result set differently than each subquery's individual limit, returning more staking reward log data than authorized?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractStateChangesByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `involvedContractIds` containing the numeric ID of the staking reward account (800) directly interpolated into SQL via `involvedContractIds.join(',')`, causing the query to return state changes for the staking reward account itself and revealing internal reward distribution state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractResultsByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `involvedContractIds` containing SQL injection payloads via the direct `involvedContractIds.join(',')` interpolation in `getContractResultsByTimestamps()` — e.g., `['1 UNION SELECT id, pending_reward FROM entity_stake--']` — to exfiltrate `pending_reward` values for all staking contracts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractLogsByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `involvedContractIds` containing SQL injection payloads via the direct `involvedContractIds.join(',')` interpolation in `getContractLogsByTimestamps()` to exfiltrate `entity_stake.pending_reward` data for staking contracts by injecting a UNION SELECT?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractIdByEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `contractIdByEvmAddressQuery` filter `DELETED <> true` — which uses `<>` instead of `IS NOT TRUE` — to match contracts where `deleted` is NULL (never explicitly set), resolving to a staking contract that appears deleted but still has accumulated `pending_reward` in `entity_stake`?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractStateByIdAndFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp` value of `0` or a very early timestamp to `getContractStateByIdAndFilters()` — switching to `contractStateTimestampQuery` — to retrieve the initial migration state of a contract's storage slots, revealing the original staking configuration set during contract deployment?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractResultsByIdAndFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all contract results with no `whereConditions` (empty array) to identify contracts with high `gas_used` values correlated with staking reward claim transactions, then target those contracts' `pending_reward` balances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractActions()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a filter with `key !== 'index'` to `getContractActions()` — which silently ignores all non-index filters — to bypass intended filtering and retrieve all contract actions at a staking reward payout timestamp, leaking the full call tree including reward amounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractStateChangesByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when `contractId` is provided, the condition becomes `(CONTRACT_ID = $N or (contractIdsQuery and migration is false))` — an OR condition — to retrieve state changes for contracts not in `involvedContractIds` as long as they match the `contractId`, leaking staking slot data for the target contract regardless of involvement?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractResultsByIdAndFiltersQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `contractResultsWithEvmAddressQuery` which selects `AMOUNT` from `contract_result` — representing HBAR transferred in the contract call — to identify contract calls where the amount matches known staking reward payout sizes, enabling precise identification of reward-receiving contracts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `RecordFileService.getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray(timestamps)` call in `getContractLogs()` — which is called with deduplicated timestamps — to perform a timing side-channel attack by measuring response time differences that reveal whether a staking reward payout occurred at a specific block?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractStateByIdAndFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `contractStateTimestampQuery` which uses `coalesce(value_written, value_read)` — falling back to `value_read` when `value_written` is null — to read the pre-write storage value of a staking-related slot, revealing the previous `stake_period_start` or `staked_node_id` value before a reward claim?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractIdByEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a brute-force enumeration of EVM addresses via `getContractIdByEvmAddress()` — which throws `NotFoundError` for no match and a generic `Error` for multiple matches — to map all deployed staking contracts by observing the different error responses, then target those with high `pending_reward`?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractResultsByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a non-array single timestamp to `getContractResultsByTimestamps()` — triggering `params = [timestamps]` and `timestampsOpAndValue = '= $1'` — combined with an `involvedContractIds` array containing the staking reward account ID (800), to retrieve contract results where the staking reward account was involved as a contract party?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/contractService.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getContractStateChangesByTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an extremely large array of timestamps to `getContractStateChangesByTimestamps()` — generating a very long `in ($1, $2, ...$N)` clause — to cause a resource exhaustion condition that delays staking reward processing, creating a window for reward theft?
